# DomainPack Protocol — per-method consumer reference

**Generated by Phase 6R-A planning** (`~/.claude/plans/swirling-knitting-lighthouse.md`).
Last updated: 2026-05-26 (post-Phase-6Q-F-2-c commit `5114b16`).

This document maps each of the 31 methods on `broker/domains/protocol.py::DomainPack`
to (a) the broker subsystem(s) that consume it, (b) the planned Phase 6R-D
sub-protocol assignment, (c) what `DefaultDomainPack` returns by default, and
(d) whether a new domain MUST override it. The intended audience is anyone
reviewing a DomainPack change, scoping a sub-protocol refactor, or onboarding
a new domain.

When in doubt, the authoritative source is the Protocol definition itself
(`broker/domains/protocol.py`). This document is **derived** from that source
plus a consumer-graph grep across `broker/`.

---

## Quick reference table

Legend:
- **Sub-proto** — proposed Phase 6R-D sub-protocol home. Frozen to align with
  Agent 1's Phase 6R Explore audit; subject to refinement during 6R-D-1.
- **Required?** — what new domain authors should plan to override.
  - **MUST** — without overriding, the broker will misbehave or refuse to
    run (raise / produce wrong-domain output).
  - **SHOULD** — override only if your domain has the feature
    (e.g. multi-agent event handlers, custom perception verbalization).
  - **MAY** — pure leaf-level customization; defaults are safe for any
    domain. Leave alone for the first cut.

| # | Method | Sub-proto | DefaultDomainPack returns | Required? | Primary consumer |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `name` (attribute) | — (composite) | `"default"` | MUST | DomainPackRegistry lookup key |
| 2 | `reflection_status_text(ctx)` | Reflection | `None` | SHOULD | `broker/components/cognitive/reflection.py:301` |
| 3 | `reflection_questions()` | Reflection | `[]` | MAY | reflection prompt builder |
| 4 | `reflection_persona()` | Reflection | `None` | MAY | reflection prompt builder |
| 5 | `reflection_trait_labels(ctx)` | Reflection | `[]` | MAY | `reflection.py:359` (batch reflection) |
| 6 | `importance_profiles()` | Memory | `{}` | SHOULD | memory engines (3 sites) |
| 7 | `compute_importance(ctx, base=0.5)` | Memory | `base` | SHOULD | memory write decisions |
| 8 | `classify_emotion(decision, ctx)` | Memory | `"neutral"` | SHOULD | memory tagging |
| 9 | `emotional_keywords()` | Memory | `{}` | SHOULD | event→emotion mapping |
| 10 | `retrieval_weights()` | Memory | `{recency: 1.0, importance: 1.0, context: 1.0}` | MAY | memory retrieval scorer |
| 11 | `memory_policy()` | Memory | `None` | SHOULD | `policy_classifier.py:73`, `initial_loader.py:101`, `universal.py:280` |
| 12 | `skill_emotion_metadata(skill_name)` | Skill | `{}` | SHOULD | `experiment_runner.py:383` |
| 13 | `extreme_actions()` | Skill | `set()` | SHOULD | ThinkingValidator extreme-actions guard |
| 14 | `action_taxonomy()` | Skill | `{}` | SHOULD | skill categorization in audit + analytics |
| 15 | `affordability_constraints()` | Skill | `{}` | MAY | financial validators |
| 16 | `event_handlers()` | Event | `{}` | SHOULD (multi-agent) | `broker/components/events/ma_manager.py:275` |
| 17 | `agent_impact_handlers()` | Event | `{}` | SHOULD (multi-agent) | `ma_manager.py:330` (per-agent impact) |
| 18 | `perception_descriptors()` | Perception | `{}` | SHOULD | `broker/components/social/perception.py` |
| 19 | `perception_field_policy()` | Perception | `{}` | MAY | perception field stripping |
| 20 | `passthrough_agent_types()` | Perception | `set()` | MAY | raw-numeric perception bypass |
| 21 | `psychological_framework()` | Governance | `FRAMEWORK_ESCAPE_HATCH` (`""`) | MUST (water) / MAY (else) | `broker/components/governance/domain_validator_dispatch.py` |
| 22 | `builtin_checks()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | validator slot configuration |
| 23 | `retrieval_policy()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | skill retrieval top_n / min_score tuning |
| 24 | `drift_policy()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | drift-monitor thresholds |
| 25 | `population_governance_policy()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | population-level governance gates |
| 26 | `policy_event_tiers()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | severity classification |
| 27 | `bridge_importance_policy()` | Governance | `{}` | MAY | MemoryBridge resolution importance |
| 28 | `csv_loader_class()` | Setup | `None` | MAY | `broker/core/agent_initializer.py` CSV loader dispatch |
| 29 | `synthetic_loader_class()` | Setup | `None` | MAY | synthetic agent generator dispatch |
| 30 | `phase_layout()` | Setup | `None` | MAY (multi-agent) | `broker/components/orchestration/phases.py` |
| 31 | `initial_memory_templates(profile)` | Setup | `[]` | MAY | initial-memory seeding |
| 32 | `mg_barrier_text(profile)` | Setup | `""` | MAY | water-specific narrative |

**Note**: `name` is an attribute, not a method, so it appears outside the
sub-protocol classification. It lives on the composite `DomainPack` itself.

---

## Sub-protocol summary (proposed Phase 6R-D layout)

| Sub-protocol | Method count | Primary consumer file(s) |
|---|---|---|
| **ReflectionPack** | 4 (#2-5) | `broker/components/cognitive/reflection.py` |
| **MemoryPack** | 6 (#6-11) | `broker/components/memory/{policy_classifier,initial_loader,universal}.py` |
| **SkillPack** | 4 (#12-15) | `broker/core/experiment_runner.py` + validators |
| **EventPack** | 2 (#16-17) | `broker/components/events/ma_manager.py` |
| **PerceptionPack** | 3 (#18-20) | `broker/components/social/perception.py` |
| **GovernancePack** | 7 (#21-27) | `broker/components/governance/*` + `broker/validators/*` |

> **Open design question for Phase 6R-D-1**: `psychological_framework()` (#21) is
> grouped under GovernancePack because its sole consumer is
> `domain_validator_dispatch.py`. But semantically it's a **framework selector**
> (which psychometric construct vocabulary applies?), not a governance-policy
> tuner like `retrieval_policy` / `drift_policy`. Phase 6R-D-1 may relocate it
> to its own `FrameworkPack` (single-method) or keep it on GovernancePack —
> decision deferred to the design pass.
| **SetupPack** | 5 (#28-32) | `broker/core/agent_initializer.py`, `broker/components/orchestration/phases.py` |

**Consumer disjointness verdict**: per Agent 1's audit, each method appears in
**exactly one** sub-protocol's consumer set — no method is read by two
broker subsystems. This is what makes the Phase 6R-D split safe; if two
sub-protocols shared a method, splitting it would cause double-dispatch.

---

## When to override what — decision flowchart for new domains

**Step 1 — MUST override (every new domain):**
- `name`: pick a unique domain identifier (lowercase, underscore-separated).
  This is the only true MUST — `DomainPackRegistry` keys on it.

**Step 2 — SHOULD override if your domain has the feature:**
- Custom narrative for reflection prompts? Override `reflection_status_text()`.
- Domain-specific events that mutate the environment? Override
  `event_handlers()` + `agent_impact_handlers()`.
- A psychometric framework (PMT / HBM / utility / cognitive_appraisal)?
  Override `psychological_framework()` and register the framework's
  constructs via `register_framework_metadata()`. **Otherwise** leave it
  as the escape-hatch `""` — the broker handles non-framework domains
  cleanly (Phase 6Q-D contract).
- Numeric→qualitative perception verbalization (e.g. "shortage_tier=H"
  rather than raw float)? Override `perception_descriptors()`.

**Step 3 — MAY override for advanced customization:**
- Memory retrieval weights, drift thresholds, governance policy tiers,
  CSV/synthetic loader classes, phase layouts.
- The defaults are safe; only override if you observe the broker's
  default behaviour conflicting with your domain's intent.

---

## Audit-trail of household-default and PMT-bias debt (cluster E + A)

Six items deferred to Phase 6R per Phase 6Q-K-3 + Phase 6Q-K-1:

| Finding | File:line | Resolution sub-phase | Approach |
|---|---|---|---|
| #15 PerceptionAwareProvider household default | `broker/components/context/providers.py:506, 508` | 6R-B-1 | `DomainPack.perception_filters()` hook |
| #14 social/config.py household defaults | `broker/components/social/config.py:121, 125, 130` | 6R-B-2 | `DomainPack.social_spec_extensions()` hook |
| #16 validate_prompt water-placeholder allowlist | `broker/tools/validate_prompt.py:85-106` | 6R-B-3 | `DomainPack.prompt_placeholder_extensions()` hook |
| #17 reflection.py household defaults | `broker/components/cognitive/reflection.py:63, 255` | 6R-B-4 | Mandatory kwarg (raise on None) |
| #3 AgentProfile typed PMT fields | `broker/core/agent_initializer.py:99-154` | 6R-C-1 | Split into `WaterAgentProfile` subclass |
| #4 SyntheticLoader.PMT_PARAMS + `H{idx}` prefix | `broker/core/agent_initializer.py:396-411, 500` | 6R-C-2 | Split into `WaterSyntheticLoader` (water namespace) |

After Phase 6R-B + 6R-C close all six, this section will be archived as
"resolved" and the Layer-3 audit closes its cluster E + A backlog.

---

## Maintenance contract

- Any new method added to `DomainPack` (e.g. a new DomainPack hook in a
  future Phase 6S) MUST be added to the quick-reference table above + the
  sub-protocol summary.
- The 4 hooks added by Phase 6R-B (perception_filters,
  social_spec_extensions, prompt_placeholder_extensions, plus the
  mandatory-kwarg refactor on reflection_context_defaults) extend
  GovernancePack to **11 methods**. Update the sub-protocol summary
  count after each sub-phase ships.
- The static gate at
  `broker/tests/test_framework_invariants.py::TestNoExcessiveInlineMockPacks`
  (Phase 6R-A) prevents this reference from drifting: any new test file
  with > 3 inline mock packs fails CI, surfacing the maintenance question
  "should this fixture come from gen_test_fixture?"
